title: AI Risk Assessment Platform
description: Thirty-question screening questionnaire for AI use case intake, regulatory path indication, governance maturity, and evidence readiness.
helperText: You can complete this gradually. Results update automatically.
helperTextSubtle: Your answers are saved while you navigate. If you want to start over, you can reset the questionnaire at the bottom of the page.

Q01.label: What sector best describes your organization?

Q02.label: In which country or countries will this AI use case be deployed?

Q03.label: What is your organization’s role for this use case?

Q04.label: What is the name of the AI use case or feature?

Q05.label: Which description best matches what the system does?

options.people_decisions: Supports or makes decisions about people

Q06.label: Is the functionality customer-facing, employee-facing, internal-only, or embedded in another product?

Q07.label: Does the use case rely on a third-party AI model, API, or foundation model provider?

Q08.label: Is this likely an AI system in scope, based on how it generates outputs, predictions, recommendations, or decisions from inputs?

Q09.label: Who is mainly affected by the outputs of this use case?

Q10.label: Could the outputs materially affect a person’s rights, access, opportunities, safety, or treatment?

Q11.label: Are children, other vulnerable persons, or sensitive populations in scope?

Q12.label: Will the system interact directly with people without it always being obvious that AI is being used?

Q13.label: What categories of data does the use case process?

Q14.label: Are training, tuning, retrieval, or prompt inputs documented well enough to describe data provenance and permitted use?

Q15.label: Are data quality checks defined for relevance, accuracy, completeness, and potential bias?

Q16.label: How are the AI outputs used in practice?

options.people_decision: Can influence decisions about people (e.g. hiring, credit approval, access to services)

Q17.label: Could a wrong output cause financial loss, denial of service, reputational damage, discrimination, or safety harm?

Q18.label: Is the use case linked to any of the following areas that may require closer regulatory screening?

Q19.label: Does the use case use biometric identification, categorization, emotion recognition, or similar inference about people?

Q20.label: Could the use case be seen as manipulating behavior, exploiting vulnerabilities, social scoring, or conducting untargeted scraping / prohibited biometric practices?

Q21.label: What level of human oversight exists before consequential outputs are used?

Q22.label: Are logging, traceability, and version records available for prompts, models, rules, and key outputs?

Q23.label: Are security controls defined for access, secrets, model/API usage, abuse prevention, and incident handling?

Q24.label: Is there monitoring for performance drift, harmful outputs, failures, and user complaints after deployment?

Q25.label: Is there a named business owner and a named risk/compliance owner for this AI use case?

Q26.label: Is this use case recorded in an AI inventory or similar governance register?

Q27.label: Have you documented the intended purpose, limitations, out-of-scope uses, and target users?

Q28.label: Has the organization assessed the third-party provider or model supplier for transparency, contractual terms, security, and documentation?

Q29.label: Have staff using or overseeing this system received AI-specific guidance or training appropriate to their role?

Q30.label: Which evidence items already exist for this use case?

notes.note_id1: This rule set is a screening and prioritization layer, not legal advice or a formal conformity assessment.
notes.note_id2: Rules are intentionally transparent and auditable so reviewers can trace outcomes to specific questionnaire responses.

R01-X03.name: High-risk domain with undefined oversight
R01-X03.recommendation1: Define and implement appropriate human oversight mechanisms before deploying AI systems in high-risk domains.

R02-C01.name: EU jurisdiction
R02-C01.recommendation1: Assess whether this system falls under the EU AI Act and identify applicable obligations based on its use and risk level.

R06-X01.name: Externally exposed AI use case
R06-X01.recommendation1: Ensure transparency by informing users that they are interacting with an AI system where applicable.

R08-C03.name: Unclear AI scope
R08-C03.note: The described functionality may be conventional automation rather than an AI system.

R08-X01.name: Possible AI scope path
R08-X01.recommendation1: Clarify and document whether the system falls within the scope of AI, as this may affect the applicability of regulatory requirements.

R08-X02.recommendation1: Establish a clear determination of whether the system qualifies as an AI system; without this, the risk assessment cannot be considered reliable.

R10-X01.recommendation1: Implement appropriate risk management, human oversight, and control measures for AI systems with material impact on individuals.

R10-X04.name: Vulnerable persons affected with material impact
R10-X04.recommendation1: Implement enhanced safeguards, oversight, and risk controls where AI systems may materially affect vulnerable persons.

R10-X06.name: Material impact with weak oversight
R10-X06.recommendation1: Define and strengthen human oversight for AI systems with material impact on people before relying on outputs in decisions or automated actions.

R11-C01.name: Children or vulnerable persons in scope

R11-C02.name: Possible children or vulnerable persons in scope

R11-C03.name: No children or vulnerable persons in scope

R13-X01.recommendation1: Ensure strict controls, legal basis, and safeguards when processing sensitive or biometric data within AI systems.

R16-X01.name: Limited oversight in consequential decisions
R16-X01.recommendation1: Strengthen human oversight for AI-supported consequential decisions and define clear review and intervention points before outputs are relied upon.

R16-X04.name: No defined oversight for consequential use
R16-X04.recommendation1: Define and implement formal human oversight before using AI outputs for consequential decisions or automated actions.

R30-X01.name: No governance evidence available
R30-X01.recommendation1: Establish core governance documentation, including use case description, data sources, risk assessment, and oversight procedures before deploying the AI system.

R002.name: Possible not in scope
R002.note: The described functionality may be conventional automation rather than an AI system.

R003.name: Potential prohibited practice indicator
R003.recommendation1: Escalate immediately for legal and compliance review before deployment.
R003.recommendation2: Document the exact use pattern and affected population.

R004.name: Biometric or emotion-related screening
R004.recommendation1: Review biometric, categorization, and emotion-related functionality against applicable AI Act restrictions and obligations.

R007.name: Direct interaction transparency check
R007.recommendation1: Consider user-facing notice or disclosure that AI is being used where appropriate.

R008.name: Children or vulnerable persons
R008.recommendation1: Add enhanced review for harms, safeguards, and communication suitable for vulnerable persons.

R009.name: Sensitive or biometric data usage
R009.recommendation1: Document legal basis, data minimization, provenance, and controls for sensitive data handling.

R010.name: Weak provenance documentation
R010.recommendation1: Create a documented data source and provenance record for training, tuning, retrieval, and prompt inputs.

R011.name: Incomplete data quality controls
R011.recommendation1: Define quality, bias, and relevance checks for data used by the AI use case.

R012.name: Limited human oversight in consequential use
R012.recommendation1: Implement human review before consequential actions or decisions are finalized.

R013.name: High potential harm
R013.recommendation1: Prioritize testing, escalation criteria, and documented risk acceptance before production use.

R014.name: Security controls missing
R014.recommendation1: Define access control, secret management, abuse prevention, and AI incident handling controls.

R016.name: Logging and traceability gap
R016.recommendation1: Implement logging for model versions, prompts/configuration, approvals, and material outputs where appropriate.

R017.name: Post-deployment monitoring gap
R017.recommendation1: Define monitoring, complaint intake, and drift or harmful-output review procedures.

R018.name: Ownership gap
R018.recommendation1: Assign a business owner and a risk/compliance owner for the AI use case.

R019.name: AI inventory gap
R019.recommendation1: Record the use case in an AI inventory or governance register.

R020.name: Purpose and limits not documented
R020.recommendation1: Document intended purpose, target users, limitations, and prohibited uses.

R021.name: Third-party supplier review missing
R021.recommendation1: Perform supplier review covering transparency, security, contractual terms, and provider documentation.

R022.name: AI literacy and training gap
R022.recommendation1: Provide role-appropriate AI training and operating guidance for staff.

R023.name: Evidence pack missing
R023.recommendation1: Create a minimum evidence pack before launch or broader rollout.

R024.name: Critical evidence gaps
R024.recommendation1: Prepare a risk assessment, oversight procedure, testing record, and monitoring plan.

R025.name: Likely minimal-risk profile
R025.note: Current answers suggest a lower-risk profile, subject to confirmation of actual deployment details.

R027.name: Unclear AI scope
R027.recommendation1: Clarify whether the functionality qualifies as an AI system and document the reasoning.

R028.name: Escalation threshold
R028.note: Immediate governance and compliance review recommended.

flags.high_risk_domain_indicator: High-risk domain (e.g. healthcare, employment, public sector, or law enforcement)
flags.external_exposure: Externally exposed system (e.g. customer-facing or embedded services)
flags.scope_unclear: AI scope unclear or not clearly defined
flags.strong_human_oversight: Strong human oversight (review and approval before action)
flags.oversight_gap: Limited human oversight in consequential use

cflags.no_defined_ovesight: No defined oversight for consequential use

evidenceChecklist.use_case_documented: AI use case description is documented
evidenceChecklist.data_source_documented: Data sources and provenance are documented
evidenceChecklist.testing_records: Testing and validation records are available
evidenceChecklist.monitoring_plan: Monitoring and performance tracking are defined

validationMessage: Please answer all required questions before viewing the results.
actionHelperLine1: Answer the questions and review Current Risk Overview.
actionHelperLine2: When ready, continue to Review results and generate a report.
